text:
*[Bug 19057] New: .bincode(breaks = numeric()) crashes with strict barrier enabled*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19057>

            Bug ID: 19057
           Summary: .bincode(breaks = numeric()) crashes with strict
                    barrier enabled
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Other
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Low-level
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3680
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3680&action=edit>
Validate the length of 'breaks' in do_bincode before use.

Seen on macOS with a build of R-devel using `--enable-strict-barrier`:

```
* thread #1, queue = 'com.apple.main-thread', stop reason = EXC_BAD_ACCESS
(code=1, address=0x1)
    frame #0: 0x00000001056f0d00 libR.dylib`bincode(x=0x0000625002d30af0,
n=<unavailable>, breaks=0x0000000000000001, nb=<unavailable>,
code=0x0000625002d30968, right=1, include_border=0) at util.c:2752:17 [opt]
   2749         if(!ISNAN(x[i])) {
   2750             lo = 0;
   2751             hi = nb1;
-> 2752             if(x[i] <  breaks[lo] || breaks[hi] < x[i] ||
   2753                (x[i] == breaks[lft ? hi : lo] && ! include_border)) ;
   2754             else {
   2755                 while(hi - lo >= 2) {
Target 0: (R) stopped.
warning: libR.dylib was compiled with optimization - stepping may behave oddly;
variables may not be available.
```

The issue here is that the length of the `breaks` argument isn't checked or
verified before access (except that the length is not NA). A candidate patch is
included.

---

```

arch           aarch64
os             darwin25.4.0
system         aarch64, darwin25.4.0
status         Under development (unstable)
major          4
minor          7.0
year           2026
month          04
day            23
svn rev        89955
language       R
version.string R Under development (unstable) (2026-04-23 r89955)
nickname       Unsuffered Consequences
```

text:
*[Bug 19057] .bincode(breaks = numeric()) crashes with strict barrier enabled*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19057>

--- Comment #1 from Kevin Ushey (<mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>) ---
A slightly less synthetic way of triggering the same crash here is something
like:

```
cut(1:5, NULL)
```

This still seems unlikely to happen in "real" user code, but I suppose it could
happen if someone made a mistake computing the breaks for their `cut()`
invocation, I suppose?
